design_philosophy.core_loop: Extend stretchable arms to grab enemies/objects, swing on poles and handles to traverse levels, use momentum-based mechanics to reach level exits with high scoring potential
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Mastery of arm-extension positioning and momentum timing versus platforming hazards; risk/reward of bonus stage hunting versus safe level completion
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as an elastic-limbed cosmic hero using unconventional arm-based physics to overcome obstacles and defeat enemies through creative manipulation of environment
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately de-emphasizes jumping and speed (contrast to Sonic) in favor of arm-based interaction mechanics; level design centered on swinging and grabbing rather than precision platforming; emphasis on momentum-based physics and environmental interaction over reflexive combat

difficulty.lives_system: Four-star health icon system; losing all stars results in life loss. Extra lives granted by collecting Ristar figures. Yellow stars restore one health unit, blue stars restore full health
difficulty.continues_system: Continues earned by completing bonus stages within time limits. Additional continues awarded based on completion speed
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based checkpoint system; bonus stages are separate challenges with time constraints
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Moderate agency through optional bonus stage completion for continues; player can choose risk/reward of pursuing hidden handles versus safe level completion

progression.unlock_mechanism: Levels unlock sequentially; hidden bonus stages accessible via special handles within each level; special codes awarded post-game based on bonus stage completion count
replayability_hooks: Bonus stage time-attack challenges for continues and post-game codes
Score maximization through exit altitude mechanics (similar to Super Mario Bros)
Hidden handle discovery for alternate stage access

world_building_techniques: Planetary setting with themed level environments (Planet Neer/Flora, etc.)
Narrative variation between Japanese and international versions (cosmic origin myth vs. paternal rescue quest)
Enemy naming suggests thematic antagonists (Kaiser Greedy, brainwashed planetary leaders)
Visual theme of cosmic/star aesthetic tied to protagonist identity

faction_indicators: Kaiser Greedy as central antagonist representing tyrannical control
Brainwashed planetary leaders as victims of mind control
Ristar as defender of innocent people's wishes
danger_signals: Kidnapping of heroic figures (father in international version)
environmental_storytelling: Treasure chests and environmental destructibility suggesting exploration-driven narrative discovery
Star-themed aesthetic reinforcing cosmic hero narrative
